name: Nordic Council

colors.symbolism: Nordic blue — sea, sky, and the shared identity of the Nordic countries

description: A blue field charged at the centre with a stylised white swan in flight; the body of the swan is formed by eight curved feather-shapes radiating around a central neck-and-head, representing the eight members of the Nordic Council.
history: The Nordic Council adopted a swan emblem in 1984 (designed by the Danish artist Steen Sauer), which was modernised in 2016 with the current swan-with-eight-feathers design by Kolbrún Anna Björnsdóttir. The flag uses the modern emblem on a Nordic-blue field. The Nordic Council itself was founded in 1952; the Nordic Council of Ministers was added in 1971.

features.symbolism: Nordic blue, evoking the sea and sky shared by the Nordic countries

A stylised white swan whose body is formed by eight curved feathers, representing the eight members of Nordic co-operation: Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway, Sweden and the autonomous territories of the Faroe Islands, Greenland and Åland.
